problem: Fenced-block state toggles on any line starting with ``` or ~~~, so a code example containing a line like echo "```" incorrectly closes the fence and exposes subsequent prose to command parsing
fix: Restrict fenced-line matching to shell-prompt ($) lines or require a following flag/argument token
evidence: [brad] 20/if strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, "```") // strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, "~~~") { inFence = !inFence } toggles on any matching prefix regardless of context / [dax] `if strings.HasPrefix(s, "atcr ")` inside `capture` matches any line starting with atcr inside a fence, not just commands / [otto] `if strings.HasPrefix(s, "atcr ")` inside a fence loop

problem: Fence toggle uses inFence=!inFence without tracking info-strings or handling unbalanced fences, leaving state true for EOF and parsing prose as commands
fix: Track fence depth or match explicit closing fences to prevent state leakage on malformed input

problem: TestReconcilerConfigSurfaceDocumented uses strings.Contains for persona:/debate:/verify:/executor:, matching those tokens in prose as readily as in a real yaml block
fix: Anchor the check to fenced-yaml lines or a start-of-line block pattern rather than a bare substring
evidence: [brad] strings.Contains(ref, block) matches prose occurrences of persona:, debate:, etc. / [dax] `strings.Contains(ref, block)` matches `persona:` anywhere in the doc, not just in a config block / [greta] if !strings.Contains(ref, block) { t.Errorf("docs/registry.md does not document the %s config block", block) } / [otto] `for _, block := range []string{"persona:", ...} { if !strings.Contains(ref, block) }`

problem: TestDocsIndexCoversEveryDoc link regex treats a titled markdown link `](x.md "title")` as target `x.md "title"`, which fails the .md suffix check and would false-fail if a future index link adds a title
fix: Strip a trailing quoted title from the captured target before the .md/anchor checks
evidence: [brad] re := regexp.MustCompile(`\]\(([^)]+)\)`) captures x.md "title", strings.HasSuffix(target, ".md") rejects it / [dax] `\]\(([^)]+)\)` captures everything up to the closing paren, including a quoted title / [greta] re := regexp.MustCompile(`\]\(([^)]+)\)`) / [otto] `target = strings.SplitN(target, "#", 2)[0]` does not handle titles

problem: TestArchitectureDocDescribesReconciler comment claims a fictional-architecture stub would fail, but it only checks 8 substrings exist anywhere, so a buzzword-sprinkled stub passes
fix: Strengthen assertion to verify stage ordering, headings, or structural markers instead of bare substring presence
evidence: [brad] for _, term := range []string{"review", "reconcile"...} { if !strings.Contains(lower, term) ... } / [dax] `strings.Contains(lower, term)` for 8 terms; a doc containing only those words in any order passes / [greta] for _, term := range []string{"review", "reconcile", "cluster", "dedupe", "confidence", "verify", "debate", "persona"} { if !strings.Contains(lower, term) { ... } / [otto] `for _, term := range []string{...} { if !strings.Contains(lower, term) }`

problem: Root README.md is folded into the atcr.yaml/Reconciler-v2/command/flag audit, so an unrelated future README edit could fail CI in a docs-audit test and surprise contributors
fix: Scope drift-token and command checks to docs/ only, or explicitly document the coupling in the test header
evidence: [brad] paths := append(docs, filepath.Join(root, "README.md")) folds root README into auditedMarkdown used by all audit tests / [dax] `auditedMarkdown` includes root README.md; all five audit tests iterate over it / [otto] `paths := append(docs, filepath.Join(root, "README.md"))` / [greta] for _, m := range regexp.MustCompile("`([^`\n]+)`").FindAllStringSubmatch(md, -1) { capture(m[1]) }
